article_title: US EV Fast-Charging Network Surpasses 71,000 Ports as Deployment Accelerates 33 Percent Year Over Year

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: US EV Fast-Charging Network Surpasses 71,000 Ports as Deployment Accelerates 33 Percent Year Over Year
content_preview.summary: The US now operates over 71,000 public DC fast chargers across 15,000 locations, with Tesla holding a narrowing majority and new entrants like Ionna reshaping the competitive landscape.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The United States now operates 71,482 public DC fast chargers spread across 15,031 locations, according to Department of Energy data reported by [CleanTechnica](https://cleantechnica.com/2026/04/09/71482-ev-fast-chargers-operating-in-the-us-now/). The milestone reflects a 33 percent year-over-year increase in the country's high-speed charging footprint, driven primarily by private-sector investment rather than federal stimulus. The expansion comes as EV charging demand continues to ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive EV charging infrastructure article with good statistical depth and cross-reference to prior EV sales coverage.
editor_notes.source_verification: CleanTechnica confirms 71,482 ports, 15,031 locations, 11,000 ultrafast 350kW+. Electrek confirms 18K+ new ports in 2025, ~30% YoY growth, Tesla share declining. Human-requested with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The 33% figure in title aligns with Electrek's ~30% sessions growth and overall deployment acceleration. Tesla 51.6% total share vs 37.6% new deployment share — the article correctly notes narrowing dominance.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Appropriately cross-references EV sales decline article.
editor_notes.originality: No prior EV charging infrastructure coverage. Complements existing EV sales article.
